Her fellow Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy's torment affected the ban, while her boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, recalls falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced from social media trolls(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second edition of the topical debate - originally billed as the first of a new series until another episode was sneaked in last week. As usual she will be inviting the studio audience - this time a venue in Norwich - to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: When Andrew Neil decided to step down from This Week back in July, the BBC decided that rather than continue with a new host, they would find a different show to take its place. And here it is - although fans of Radio 5 Live will already be familiar with Brexitcast, which has become hugely popular since its launch in 2017. This TV version sees the same team, Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason, engaging in light-hearted gossip about all the latest goings-on in the process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley meets the volunteer teams who carry out rescues in the caves and mines of the Peak District in Derbyshire. Rav Wilding has the latest appeals and CCTV footage(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, to see a commercial space, and in Kent meets a couple who are returning to property development after taking a few years out. Dion Dublin meets a husband and wife team at a split-level flat in London(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Trading Standards Officers search the home of a loan shark and find a safe containing thousands of pounds. They also force a rogue builder to pay compensation to his elderly victims. Narrated by Harry Hepple(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles introduces more footage of near misses caught on CCTV, meeting the people who survived the serious accidents. This episode features a paraglider who crashed into a wall in the Peak District, a cyclist who smashed face-first onto the pavement witnessed by her son, and a cameraman who while filming a storm almost ended up a victim himself(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles starts this programme at the British Bird of Prey centre in the National Botanic Garden of Wales and asks the people of Carmarthenshire to help identify a mystery object. The red and blue teams then go shopping with experts Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt, hoping to find items of value to take to auction in Cardiff(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara tries to be the bigger person when she finds out about Daniel and Becky's plans, while Valerie helps her neighbour when his selfless act is tarnished by the ultimate betrayal(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson crosses the Solent to property shop on the Isle of Wight with a couple who have a £750,000 budget to find their dream island home with holiday-let potential. During his journey, the presenter also gets an intimate glimpse into 19th-century royal life when he visits Osbourne House, one of Queen Victoria's favourite homes(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the rolling Cotswold Hills in their 1970s off-roader. There are ancient artefacts, dry stone walls and lots and lots of sheep - but who will win out at the final auction in Devizes, Wiltshire?(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich are joined by Arit Anderson to create an all-year-round garden in Byfleet, Surrey, for stylish octogenarian Gay to enjoy the birds and remember her late husband. As ever, the two design teams go head to head, so it remains to be seen whther newcomer Arit's garden, which uses repeating circle motifs to create interest in an otherwise small and featureless space and incorporates a curved wooden seat surrounded by bright flowers, will triumph(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England goalkeeper David James, Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Elinor Barker, Ryder Cup golfer Andy Sullivan and rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian is unsure how to deal with Bobby and things only get worse when Max tells him he has agreed to let his son stay with him. Chantelle makes it to the final two in the competition - only to face a big decision. After her operation, Jean is overjoyed when Kat gets Stacey on the phone. But then Daniel arrives with devastating news(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The battle for a semi-final place reaches its climax as the four celebrities are tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by last year's finalists Martin Bayfield and Spencer Matthews and champion John Partridge. Only the best two contestants can come one step closer to winning the title of Celebrity MasterChef 2019. For those who don't make the grade, the journey ends here(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Mike and Karen try to get their relationship back on track after Mike's crab stall misdemeanour, while he finally finds Bigsy sleeping rough at the castle after going Awol. Meanwhile after the police visit Marion about the break in next door, Karen heads back to the salon to discover that Geraldine is under suspicion over the mystery of what happened to the contents of Mrs Bookham's handbag. Comedy written by Benidorm's Derren Litten, starring Jason Manford, Catherine Tyldesley and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: Room 101 - Extra Storage
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel show presented by Frank Skinner, with Lucy Porter, Romesh Ranganathan and Judge Robert Rinder competing to have their pet hates consigned to Room 101, with topics including lavish proposals, school reunions and New Year's Eve(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Steve McAndrew brings his dying father to London so he can spend his final days in a nearby hospice, but how much time he can spend with his parent is anyone's guess - the Ucos team is hard at work investigating the cold case murder of 55-year-old interpreter Agnes Bradley. A DNA sample provides a link to a rebellious teenager, and the team wonders if the killer could be related to him. But a whole new can of worms is opened when investigators discover the lad's mother was raped - could the same person have killed Agnes? Gerry and Steve hope that interviewing her friends from a local chess club may shed new light on the matter, but it's a possible professional issue that could hold the key to unlocking the mystery. Denis Lawson and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Adam Byatt and Niklas Ekstedt, while Jane Parkinson selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: Mary heads to the hustle and bustle of Marrakesh to explore every side of Moroccan cuisine. She rustles up some quick dishes inspired by the aromatic flavours and smells of the medina quarter, from the mounds of olives and heady spices at the markets to the slow-cooked tagines prepared in the heart of a family kitchen and the high-end delights of Moha Fedal. one of the country's greatest chefs. Mary brings the fragrant flavours home, cooking a speedy lamb tagine, a spicy carrot hummus and a fresh summer salad(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Norwich City v Manchester City and Liverpool v Newcastle United in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents as the hunt for bargains heads to Oswestry, Shropshire, where experts Jonathan Pratt and John Cameron assist two teams of friends competing to buy three items in the hope of making the most profit at auction. Natasha also heads to Hawarden, Flintstone, to visit Gladstone's Library, Britain's only prime ministerial library, where she reveals what a wheelbarrow and a former prime minister have in common(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a retired detective and his wife to look for a home in Gloucestershire on a £500,000 budget, and goes behind the scenes ahead of the Badminton Horse Trials(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades salvages three items bound for the skip in Walsall, featuring a Lloyd Loom-style laundry basket, a reclining chair and an oak writing bureau(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie is joined by Steven Thompson and Leanne Crichton present the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points(n)

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005)
programme.desc: Inventor Wallace and his canine sidekick Gromit have set up a pest-control service, and are called in to stop rabbits overrunning a garden show. However, the pair are overwhelmed when a destructive giant bunny arrives on the scene, while an interfering aristocrat contrives to make matters worse. Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A special programme focusing on Strictly Come Dancing's professional dancers, looking back at their journeys to reach the ballroom and some of their best moments from the show. The programme also offers viewers a behind-the-scenes look at life on Strictly, revealing some of the show's most guarded secrets(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A Radio 2 edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Jo Whiley, Gary Davies, Ken Bruce, Angela Scanlon, Craig Charles, Trevor Nelson, the Rev Kate Bottley and Jason Mohammad, and a special performance from Rick Astley. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: The classical music celebration continues live from the Royal Albert Hall. Sakari Oramo conducts the BBC Symphony Orchestra and Chorus and the BBC Singers through an energetic programme, including the Overture to Offenbach's Orpheus in the Underworld, Grainger's Marching Song of Democracy, Harold Arlen's Somewhere Over the Rainbow and Gershwin's I Got Rhythm. The event is then brought to its familiar and rousing close with Rule, Britannia!, Land of Hope and Glory and Jerusalem. Presented by Katie Derham(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Norwich City v Manchester City at Carrow Road and Liverpool v Newcastle United at Anfield. The Canaries had not beaten their opponents at home since way back in August 2001 in the old First Division, although the two most recent Premier League encounters between the sides on this ground finished in very creditable 0-0 draws. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Burnley at the Amex Stadium, Manchester United v Leicester City at Old Trafford, Sheffield United v Southampton at Bramall Lane, Tottenham Hotspur v Crystal Palace at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Chelsea at Molineux(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a flat in Forest Hill, London, and two terraced houses in Leeds and Nantwich, Cheshire, before speaking to their new owners(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The officers investigate the sale of knives to under-18s. They also catch up with a Lamborghini-driving fraudster who has so many aliases he loses track of who he is supposed to be(n)

programme.desc: An animal-lover falls down a sewer trying to rescue his dog and a 60-year-old woman trying to row the Atlantic finds herself in jeopardy when her boat fills with water(n)

programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and Nick Hall help two teams scour Nottingham racecourse for items, while presenter Charlie Ross tests people's knowledge of hallmarks(n)

programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin visits Shropshire in search of a country home for a pair of free spirits who have a £300,000 budget with which to finally lay down some roots. Jonnie also gets in the saddle on a ride through the Shropshire hills with a bespoke bike builder(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue

Daniel tries to distract Jean ahead of her operation, Chantelle's nerves threaten her chances of winning the competition and Phil overhears Sonia speaking to Bianca about barely seeing her and sends them an anonymous gift(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox return with the consumer show. They investigate allegations about a safety device in millions of homes and rank energy companies on cost, complaints and customer service. Pixie Lott exposes how high street chains are failing on the basics of recycling, and in Rogue Traders, Matt confronts a gardening firm whose elaborate lies have left customers thousands of pounds out of pocket(n)

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Having made it through a very wet winter, Sweeny now focuses on new life on the croft as he heads into spring. It is one of his favourite times of the year, and it's all go as one of his sows goes into labour, but this is one that he has to keep a close eye on. He also deals with the challenges of lambing; a time of very little sleep and plenty of early starts. With over 50 already lambed he's half way there, but his early morning checks prove vital as he finds out when he needs to give help to one of his sheep which is struggling. This isn't the only sheep he has to help. Sweeny lives on one of the most rugged coastlines in Britain, and although it's beautiful it can be very dangerous for the animals as he knows all to well. He calls the local mountain rescue in to help one of his sheep distressed on the cliff edge(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of day two of the St Leger Festival, including the 2.40 DFS Park Hill Stakes and the 3.45 William Hill May Hill Stakes, plus races at 2.10 and 3.15. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Kevin Blake, reports by Luke Harvey, Sally Ann Grassick and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Mike, Tori, Sarah and Aaron work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jessie and Al reminisce about their past, Moira and Nate's plans are thwarted, and Doug is caught red-handed(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: With nearly four million fraud offences in England and Wales last year, criminals appear to be increasingly high-tech. Scammers stole over a billion pounds from UK bank customers in 2018 using a variety of cons - including impersonating public officials and government websites. Reporter Helen Skelton investigates, and reveals for the first time how she became a victim, losing tens of thousands of pounds in the process(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon admits his frustrations, Jessie gives in to temptation, and Mandy makes an appearance(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul returns to meet more children who are receiving life-changing treatment at Great Ormond Street Hospital in London, including five-year-old chatterbox Daniel, who has been confined to a wheelchair for two weeks after being struck down out of the blue by juvenile idiopathic arthritis. Paul also speaks to 14-year-old Reagan, who has scoliosis, a progressive condition that has left her with an ever-increasing curvature in her spine, and 17-year-old Gavriel, who has Duchenne muscular dystrophy(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Great American Trail
programme.desc: Billy visits Kearny, New Jersey, where he discovers it was the Scots who brought football to America. He then embarks on a pilgrimage to the Martin Guitar Company - the factory that has built guitars for some of the biggest rock stars on the planet, including Billy's hero Hank Williams. He also ventures to the Eastern State Penitentiary, which was once America's most notorious prison and the one-time residence of legendary gangster Al Capone(n)

programme.title: The Game Show Serial Killer: Police Tapes
programme.desc: Susanna Reid tells the story of the search for Pembrokeshire serial killer John Cooper, who murdered four people in Wales between 1985 and 1989, but avoided capture for more than 20 years - until the combination of a new detective team, advances in forensic science finally brought him to justice in 2011. His crimes began in 1985, when he shot a brother and sister at their farm near Milford Haven. Four years later, he killed a couple as they walked along a coastal path. However, it would be his 1989 appearance the TV game show Bullseye that would eventually prove his undoing(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Charlotte sets out to avoid Sidney, until a serious accident forces them together and to reappraise each other. When Tom brings a new doctor to the town, he and Lady Denham clash, while Clara makes a bold move in her bid to outplay Sir Edward and Esther. Drama adapted from the unfinished novel by Jane Austen, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Anne Reid, Charlotte Spencer, Kris Marshall and Kate Ashfield(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: With nearly four million fraud offences in England and Wales last year, criminals appear to be increasingly high-tech. Scammers stole over a billion pounds from UK bank customers in 2018 using a variety of cons - including impersonating public officials and government websites. Reporter Helen Skelton investigates, and reveals for the first time how she became a victim, losing tens of thousands of pounds in the process(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Rachel, Chris, Judith and Adam work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity dreads what is to come, Moira has second thoughts, and Mandy catches up with the Dingles(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ali begs Dr Gaddas for a diazepam prescription, but she is not fooled and urges him to get help for his addiction. Chesney uncovers Bernie's positive pregnancy test scam, and Kate asks Sophie if she'd like to go travelling with her(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective is faced with a difficult case when he investigates the discovery of a body in a well, and he also has to contend with a new boss, Chief Superintendent Moody, who is dubious as to Lewis' motivation for coming out of retirement. As investigations into the human remains in the well proceed, an avant-garde artist's body is also discovered and at first glance it looks like a tragic drugs overdose, but Hathaway and Lewis quickly recognise the tell tale signs of murder(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: In light of their momentous kiss, Cole wants to talk to his ex Natalie about the future direction of their relationship. However, a spanner is thrown in his plans after he bumps into new Assistant District Attorney Erica Malick, who he immediately ruffles feathers with. Meanwhile, after being kicked out of the house for Trish's girl's night, Murtaugh finds friendship in an unexpected place with Elliot, the witness that Cole sticks him with. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans, Seann William Scott and Nishi Munshi(n)

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: In the north of England severe flooding, the worst in 70 years, brings chaos to the region. Standard emergency services are overwhelmed, and numerous volunteer rescue teams are brought in to assist in evacuating members of the public from their flooded homes. In Co Kerry, the local mountain rescue team is called to Torc Mountain and a steep path known as 'cardiac steps' to attend a walker complaining of chest pains. In the Cairngorm mountains, members of the local rescue team comb the third region to find a lost walker who has not returned home after heading out on a trek(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Lifeguard Liam from Newcastle, barista Jonathon from Newport, hairdresser Nick from Pontefract and bagpiper Glenn from Glasgow all vie for the attentions of 30 single women, intent on capturing their interest and securing a date. However, first they must get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Host Paddy McGuinness monitors their progress and reviews the dating action from last week's couples on the Isle of Fernando's(n)

programme.title: Ideal World
programme.desc: Carpet cleaning solutions(n)

programme.title: The Level
programme.desc: Detective Sergeant Nancy Devlin has a secret double life - her exemplary police career masks a covert attachment to shady businessman and drugs trafficker Frank Le Saux. She has remained loyal to Frank from childhood, as he was the father figure she craved, so plays a dangerous game to ensure that he always remains off the police radar. However, she finds herself at the centre of an investigation that puts her at risk of exposure and sees her stalked by a killer intent on destroying her. Drama, starring Karla Crome and Philip Glenister(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits the Languedoc region, famous for its wine and the hearty, meaty dish cassoulet, and stops at a roadside fruit stall owned by a couple of British expats, before creating an unusual dish using melon and onion rings. He also wanders round a museum dedicated to Citroen 2CVs and cooks a steak on a barbecue in the blazing sunshine(n)

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Former royal butler Paul Burrell puts on a dinner party for four strangers, who have to work out the identity of their mystery celebrity host with the help of a few cryptic clues - otherwise they will be doing the washing up. Hosted by Brian McFadden(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of day four of the St Leger Festival, including the 3.35 William Hill St Leger Stakes, which follows races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.00. Plus, a race from 2.05 at Chester, and 2.45 at Chelmsford. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Kevin Blake, reports by Luke Harvey, Mick Fitzgerald, Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another bunch of domestic mishaps, captured in video form by viewers. This time, a Brexit victory dance ends in disaster, a runaway car demolishes a wall, and a dog eats Lady Gaga(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking contestants Michael, Erin, and Stewart to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Russell Kane, Leonie Elliott, Kevin Clifton and Bonnie Langford pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions
programme.desc: Ant and Dec welcome more champions of past series back to the stage, along with winners from overseas variants of the show. This week's visitors include a death-defying performance from Alexandr Magala, whose sword-swallowing skills made him a worthy winner of Russia's Got Talent. Among the old favourites putting in a return appearance are series four dancers Twist and Pulse and Collabro, the musical theatre singing group who were crowned champions of series eight. Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams are on hand to pass judgement on the acts(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined for the launch of a new series by Downton Abbey stars Elizabeth McGovern and Michelle Dockery, who discuss reviving the period drama for a new movie. Other guests include Stephen Fry, Craig David and Martin Freeman, currently starring in true-life crime drama A Confession. Plus, Charli XCX and Christine and The Queens perform together in the studio(n)

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: Holly Willoughby hosts the sports-based comedy panel show. Talk show host Jonathan Ross, football pundit Chris Kamara and Coronation Street's Jack P Shepherd (David Platt) join team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh this week, with regular panellists Romesh Ranganathan and Seann Walsh also on hand to provide the laughs(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon admits his frustrations, Jessie gives in to temptation, and Mandy makes an appearance(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul returns to meet more children who are receiving life-changing treatment at Great Ormond Street Hospital in London, including five-year-old chatterbox Daniel, who has been confined to a wheelchair for two weeks after being struck down out of the blue by juvenile idiopathic arthritis. Paul also speaks to 14-year-old Reagan, who has scoliosis, a progressive condition that has left her with an ever-increasing curvature in her spine, and 17-year-old Gavriel, who has Duchenne muscular dystrophy(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Great American Trail
programme.desc: Billy visits Kearny, New Jersey, where he discovers it was the Scots who brought football to America. He then embarks on a pilgrimage to the Martin Guitar Company - the factory that has built guitars for some of the biggest rock stars on the planet, including Billy's hero Hank Williams. He also ventures to the Eastern State Penitentiary, which was once America's most notorious prison and the one-time residence of legendary gangster Al Capone(n)

programme.title: The Game Show Serial Killer: Police Tapes
programme.desc: Susanna Reid tells the story of the search for Pembrokeshire serial killer John Cooper, who murdered four people in Wales between 1985 and 1989, but avoided capture for more than 20 years - until the combination of a new detective team, advances in forensic science finally brought him to justice in 2011. His crimes began in 1985, when he shot a brother and sister at their farm near Milford Haven. Four years later, he killed a couple as they walked along a coastal path. However, it would be his 1989 appearance the TV game show Bullseye that would eventually prove his undoing(n)

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.desc: Snot is rejected after professing his love for Hayley, so Roger decides to give him a new love interest - by pretending to be Steve's female cousin. Meanwhile, Stan tries to live like a gentleman of the 1960s, complete with dark suits and dry martinis(n)

programme.desc: The employees worry when their new district manager arrives. Elsewhere, Amy and Jonah search for ways to raise money so that no one is fired(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan decides to re-upholster some chairs, smuggling some material used for the bus seats out of the depot. However, Inspector Blake unexpectedly calls in at his home, resulting in the need to rapidly camoflage the furniture. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.desc: Tricia is over the moon as she and Mike finally declare their love for one another - but gets drawn into a case of domestic violence before she has chance to celebrate, and ends up fighting for her life. Starring Clare Calbraith and Jason Durr(n)

programme.desc: Amos lets slip to Jack that he and Sarah are planning a surprise for Annie at Christmas. Jack rants to Joe about how Frank Tate is trying to put him out of business(n)

programme.desc: Colin charms Raquel and she agrees to see him on her evening off. Deirdre has enough of customers laughing at her new uniform, and Reg finds a buyer for Maureen's house(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are called away from a team-building exercise to investigate a fatal explosion at a haulage yard in Calham Cross. The property is owned by Territorial Army commanding officer Matt Parkes, the father of a businessman whose ruthlessness in the boardroom is matched only by his behaviour toward women. To complicate matters, the village is also home to a peace protester and a grieving mother - and Barnaby has to decide if any of these locals is capable of arson and murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with Tim Pigott-Smith, Joseph Millson, Niamh Cusack, Judy Parfitt and Nick Fletcher(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Bertie's circumstances change suddenly, leaving Edith uncertain whether to risk telling him the truth or keep her past a secret at all costs. Mary wonders if she can ever make peace with her sister as hostilities escalate, Spratt shows some hidden talents and Molesley's first day as a teacher does not go according to plan. Mrs Patmore's bed and breakfast attracts unwanted attention, leaving her in need of the Crawleys' help(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Disaster strikes on the set of a TV sitcom when the producer is killed in cold blood - leaving Jessica to determine whether one of the show's actors might have done the deed, or if somebody else bears a grudge. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The dead body of a teenage boy is found in a reservoir near a remote rural timber community. Vera and her team investigate, and discover that the boy had already been missing for three days prior to his death. However, the case is complicated by an apparent conspiracy of silence, as many of the townsfolk refuse to cooperate with the police investigation. A complex web of relationships between the victim's friends and family slowly emerges, with many dark secrets that could have driven someone to kill(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks agrees to help old friend and partner Jimmy Valentine with an undercover drug sting, but when the bust goes wrong and a dealer ends up dead, suspicion falls on the officers. As Devlin pushes for the truth, the spotlight turns on Brooks' dark past, and a 10-year-old case involving no witnesses and a large quantity of missing heroin. Guest starring Hustle's Robert Glenister, former Rising Damp star Don Warrington and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral), alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Two children go on trial for killing a toddler, each blaming the other, and the case hinges on whether forensic evidence can prove who is guilty of strangling the boy to death. CPS director George Castle defies his superiors and takes instruction from the victim's mother, who has a surprising point of view on the crime. Drama, guest starring Deborah Findlay (Cranford), with Bill Paterson(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: A smooth-talking new tenant called Seymour, whose aristocratic veneer conceals the soul of an out-and-out crook, tries to con Rigsby out of his life savings. Classic comedy, with Leonard Rossiter and Henry McGee(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Tricia Summerbee tries to get back to normal following her near-fatal heart attack, despite Mike's concerns that she has returned to work too soon. Jason Durr, Clare Calbraith and William Simons star(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth gives Eric a hard time about selling the car to Mark. Jayesh recovers from his attack, and Jack wants to know what surprise Sarah is planning for Annie(n)

programme.desc: Jack is furious with Sarah for inviting Leonard to stay. Kim tries to involve Kathy in the running of the stables. Zoe arrives home and wants Kim to fill her in on all the gossip(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise turns to Don for comfort, Brendan invites Deirdre for a drink after work, and Steve and Liz are alerted to the break-in and check the house to see what has been taken(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Julian Fellowes bids farewell to the Crawleys with one last festive special, catching up with the Abbey's residents at the tail-end of 1925. Mary endeavours to build bridges, but Edith's secret continues to pose a threat. As Henry settles into the role of husband, scars left by Charlie's death are slow to heal, while Carson struggles to come to terms with developments that prove even he is not invulnerable to change. Spratt's moonlighting venture is threatened by the conniving of Denker, and Daisy may have missed her chance with an admirer. Isobel receives devastating news, while the day that Anna and Bates have long-since waited for arrives(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is on hand to help a puppet designer when he runs into difficulties with his own creations - but after a security guard is killed, she finds herself on the trail of a murderer. Detective drama, guest starring Malcolm in the Middle's Bryan Cranston, with Angela Lansbury(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle investigates what he believes to be a burglary at the headquarters of a multinational company, and the murder of a Home Guard member during a large-scale exercise on the Sussex Downs soon leads him to believe the firm are conducting covert business with the Nazis - and that his former police sergeant may be involved. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell(n)

programme.title: Hidden Treasure
programme.desc: John Thaw plays a widower and ruthlessly successful businessman enjoying the fruits of his labours when a knock at the door turns his world upside down, and he finds himself custodian of an eight-year-old grand-daughter he never knew he had. Shame-faced and irascible by turns, he is no match for the youngster and, frustrated by the authorities, sets out to find her father - only to make a discovery about himself. Feature-length drama, also starring Dominique Jackson and Wil Johnson(n)

programme.title: Carry On Don't Lose Your Head (1966)
programme.desc: A cunning English nobleman puts his talent for disguise to good use by rescuing aristocrats from execution during the French Revolution. However, the leader of the secret police is determined to unmask the elusive hero spiriting the nobility out from under the guillotine. Comedy spoof of the Scarlet Pimpernel story, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims, Jim Dale and Charles Hawtrey(n)

programme.title: Great Estates of Scotland
programme.desc: Exploring Kincardine castle, and following the current owners as they organise dinners and fishing and shooting parties, and attend the oldest Highland Games in the world(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. The family warms to the French way of life, but Charley is unhappy with Mariette's flirting - and after a little Dutch courage, decides to take matters into his own hands. Drama, starring Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop busies himself organising a christening while awaiting the birth of his first grandchild - and still finds time to cast a beady eye over the contents of an elderly widow's home. Whimsical rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Preparations get under way for the christening, while Primrose loses her heart to a very confused vicar. Pop gets a black eye, Edith Pilchester tackles a gang of roving Teddy boys and Mademoiselle Dupont begins to feel ill. Drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin What, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The owner of local country magazine Midsomer Life becomes a suspect when a man's body is discovered in woodland, and it is established the victim had been having an affair with his wife. As Barnaby and Jones delve into the case, another murder forces them to reassess their inquiries. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Simon Williams(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A magician's assistant drops dead on stage, poisoned by the toxins from a tropical frog. While Barnaby and Jones begin their investigations, tempers flare in the local community between Ernest, a firm believer in a mystical cult, and his former friend Aloysius - whose background in the jungles of South America makes him the prime suspect for the murder. Starring John Nettles, Ronald Pickup and Stuart Wilson(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris)(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The discovery of a 2,000-year-old body provides fresh evidence in a five-year-old unsolved missing persons case, so Morse and Thursday take up the investigation, discovering a possible link to a local pagan ritual to celebrate the harvest. The investigation leads them to a nuclear power station, where they face a conspiracy of silence concerning an experimental new reactor and a potential disaster that could devastate Oxford. Guest starring Sheila Hancock, widow of original Inspector Morse star John Thaw(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. programme.desc: Tricia is over the moon as she and Mike finally declare their love for one another - but gets drawn into a case of domestic violence before she has chance to celebrate, and ends up fighting for her life. Starring Clare Calbraith and Jason Durr(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Amos lets slip to Jack that he and Sarah are planning a surprise for Annie at Christmas. Jack rants to Joe about how Frank Tate is trying to put him out of business(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Colin fancies Raquel but Des warns him off. Curly enjoys being in charge and gives his troops an inspection. Audrey gets fed up of Alf following her around all day(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Colin charms Raquel and she agrees to see him on her evening off. Deirdre has enough of customers laughing at her new uniform, and Reg finds a buyer for Maureen's house(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are called away from a team-building exercise to investigate a fatal explosion at a haulage yard in Calham Cross. The property is owned by Territorial Army commanding officer Matt Parkes, the father of a businessman whose ruthlessness in the boardroom is matched only by his behaviour toward women. To complicate matters, the village is also home to a peace protester and a grieving mother - and Barnaby has to decide if any of these locals is capable of arson and murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with Tim Pigott-Smith, Joseph Millson, Niamh Cusack, Judy Parfitt and Nick Fletcher(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Bertie's circumstances change suddenly, leaving Edith uncertain whether to risk telling him the truth or keep her past a secret at all costs. Mary wonders if she can ever make peace with her sister as hostilities escalate, Spratt shows some hidden talents and Molesley's first day as a teacher does not go according to plan. Mrs Patmore's bed and breakfast attracts unwanted attention, leaving her in need of the Crawleys' help(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Disaster strikes on the set of a TV sitcom when the producer is killed in cold blood - leaving Jessica to determine whether one of the show's actors might have done the deed, or if somebody else bears a grudge. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The dead body of a teenage boy is found in a reservoir near a remote rural timber community. Vera and her team investigate, and discover that the boy had already been missing for three days prior to his death. However, the case is complicated by an apparent conspiracy of silence, as many of the townsfolk refuse to cooperate with the police investigation. A complex web of relationships between the victim's friends and family slowly emerges, with many dark secrets that could have driven someone to kill(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks agrees to help old friend and partner Jimmy Valentine with an undercover drug sting, but when the bust goes wrong and a dealer ends up dead, suspicion falls on the officers. As Devlin pushes for the truth, the spotlight turns on Brooks' dark past, and a 10-year-old case involving no witnesses and a large quantity of missing heroin. Guest starring Hustle's Robert Glenister, former Rising Damp star Don Warrington and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral), alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Two children go on trial for killing a toddler, each blaming the other, and the case hinges on whether forensic evidence can prove who is guilty of strangling the boy to death. CPS director George Castle defies his superiors and takes instruction from the victim's mother, who has a surprising point of view on the crime. Drama, guest starring Deborah Findlay (Cranford), with Bill Paterson(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill(n)

programme.desc: Stan faces a cash crisis so his mother lets out the front room to Luxton & District buses' new transport manager(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A series of complaints prompts Inspector Blake to devise a new exit and entry system for the buses to leave and return to the depot. However, Stan falls foul of the new guidelines. Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis star(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: A smooth-talking new tenant called Seymour, whose aristocratic veneer conceals the soul of an out-and-out crook, tries to con Rigsby out of his life savings. Classic comedy, with Leonard Rossiter and Henry McGee(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mariette and Charley locate Pieter and beg him to stay, while Pop faces hostility from the villagers. Primrose does her best to make an impression on the Reverend Candy. David Jason and Pam Ferris star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Tricia Summerbee tries to get back to normal following her near-fatal heart attack, despite Mike's concerns that she has returned to work too soon. Elsewhere, Ventress investigates a murder in the village - and gets a shock when the prime suspect turns out to be a thief he certified dead more than 15 years earlier. Jason Durr, Clare Calbraith and William Simons star(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth gives Eric a hard time about selling the car to Mark. Jayesh recovers from his attack, and Jack wants to know what surprise Sarah is planning for Annie(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is furious with Sarah for inviting Leonard to stay. Kim tries to involve Kathy in the running of the stables. Zoe arrives home and wants Kim to fill her in on all the gossip(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise is upset that to Hanif she is nothing but a bit of fun. Curly is annoyed when Reg alters the lunch rota so he can be with Maureen(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise turns to Don for comfort, Brendan invites Deirdre for a drink after work, and Steve and Liz are alerted to the break-in and check the house to see what has been taken(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Julian Fellowes bids farewell to the Crawleys with one last festive special, catching up with the Abbey's residents at the tail-end of 1925. Mary endeavours to build bridges, but Edith's secret continues to pose a threat. As Henry settles into the role of husband, scars left by Charlie's death are slow to heal, while Carson struggles to come to terms with developments that prove even he is not invulnerable to change. Spratt's moonlighting venture is threatened by the conniving of Denker, and Daisy may have missed her chance with an admirer. Isobel receives devastating news, while the day that Anna and Bates have long-since waited for arrives(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is on hand to help a puppet designer when he runs into difficulties with his own creations - but after a security guard is killed, she finds herself on the trail of a murderer. Detective drama, guest starring Malcolm in the Middle's Bryan Cranston, with Angela Lansbury(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle investigates what he believes to be a burglary at the headquarters of a multinational company, and the murder of a Home Guard member during a large-scale exercise on the Sussex Downs soon leads him to believe the firm are conducting covert business with the Nazis - and that his former police sergeant may be involved. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell(n)

programme.title: Hidden Treasure
programme.desc: John Thaw plays a widower and ruthlessly successful businessman enjoying the fruits of his labours when a knock at the door turns his world upside down, and he finds himself custodian of an eight-year-old grand-daughter he never knew he had. Shame-faced and irascible by turns, he is no match for the youngster and, frustrated by the authorities, sets out to find her father - only to make a discovery about himself. Feature-length drama, also starring Dominique Jackson and Wil Johnson(n)

programme.title: Carry On Don't Lose Your Head (1966)
programme.desc: A cunning English nobleman puts his talent for disguise to good use by rescuing aristocrats from execution during the French Revolution. However, the leader of the secret police is determined to unmask the elusive hero spiriting the nobility out from under the guillotine. Comedy spoof of the Scarlet Pimpernel story, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims, Jim Dale and Charles Hawtrey(n)

programme.title: Great Estates of Scotland
programme.desc: Exploring Kincardine castle, and following the current owners as they organise dinners and fishing and shooting parties, and attend the oldest Highland Games in the world(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Mum and Olive are suffering from the flu _ leaving Stan and Arthur to do all the chores(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan smuggles some tools out of work to help Arthur to get his motorcycle working(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan smuggles a woman up to his room, making sure the lights are turned down low so she cannot see how shabby it is - but an unscheduled appearance by her irate father soon puts paid to his dreams of romance. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. The family warms to the French way of life, but Charley is unhappy with Mariette's flirting - and after a little Dutch courage, decides to take matters into his own hands. Drama, starring Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop busies himself organising a christening while awaiting the birth of his first grandchild - and still finds time to cast a beady eye over the contents of an elderly widow's home. Whimsical rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Preparations get under way for the christening, while Primrose loses her heart to a very confused vicar. Pop gets a black eye, Edith Pilchester tackles a gang of roving Teddy boys and Mademoiselle Dupont begins to feel ill. Drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin What, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The owner of local country magazine Midsomer Life becomes a suspect when a man's body is discovered in woodland, and it is established the victim had been having an affair with his wife. As Barnaby and Jones delve into the case, another murder forces them to reassess their inquiries. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Simon Williams(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A magician's assistant drops dead on stage, poisoned by the toxins from a tropical frog. While Barnaby and Jones begin their investigations, tempers flare in the local community between Ernest, a firm believer in a mystical cult, and his former friend Aloysius - whose background in the jungles of South America makes him the prime suspect for the murder. Starring John Nettles, Ronald Pickup and Stuart Wilson(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle investigates what he believes to be a burglary at the headquarters of a multinational company, and the murder of a Home Guard member during a large-scale exercise on the Sussex Downs soon leads him to believe the firm are conducting covert business with the Nazis - and that his former police sergeant may be involved. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris)(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The discovery of a 2,000-year-old body provides fresh evidence in a five-year-old unsolved missing persons case, so Morse and Thursday take up the investigation, discovering a possible link to a local pagan ritual to celebrate the harvest. The investigation leads them to a nuclear power station, where they face a conspiracy of silence concerning an experimental new reactor and a potential disaster that could devastate Oxford. Guest starring Sheila Hancock, widow of original Inspector Morse star John Thaw(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: The lure of easy money beckons once again as Terry and Arthur appreciate the commercial possibilities of cheap tobacco, but it is not long before the would-be smugglers are all at sea. Comedy, starring George Cole, Dennis Waterman and Ray Winstone(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle teams up with a Hong Kong cop, who is in London trailing a Triad gang supplying drugs to US army personnel in Germany while operating an international protection racket. Meanwhile, Bodie goes undercover as a down-and-out in a squat where two young drug addicts have died. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's holiday is disrupted by the escape of a violent psychopath from prison. Tim Cook, dubbed 'The Leopard' by the press has vowed murderous revenge on the detective inspector for his earlier capture, and has friends on the outside prepared to harbour him while he plans his attack. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A small town becomes the scene of a murder, but the medical examiner meets a wall of silence when he arrives to investigate the suspicious death(n)

programme.desc: CI5 investigates a consortium building a lethal fighter plane in eastern Europe, that appears willing to kill to supress publication of the aircraft's appalling safetly record. Lynda Bellingham guest stars, with Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.desc: The son of a chemical company's founder is blackmailed by his uncle, who wants to force him into selling the business. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Roddy McDowall and Ida Lupino(n)

programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes the group to The Royal Hotel in Sheerness, Kent, where partners Sarah Lewry and Gareth Segrove believe they offer great value for money(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Adhurst Yurts in Petersfield, Hampshire, where owner Alison Lubbock surprises the guests by revealing they are going to spend a night under the stars(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the final day of the competition, the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The Clumber Park Hotel kicks off the questioning and hosts Rajesh and Yuanee want Alison to dish the dirt on her cleanliness rating(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Lyndsey and Paul from Glasgow are newly engaged and want to kick start the next chapter of their lives together by buying a family holiday home in the area around Oliva in eastern Spain. Ben Hillman leaves no stone unturned to find property contenders for their budget of £50,000(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Flamingo chick Marley faces a tough childhood when mother Calypso loses interest in her duties - leaving dad Rum to do the work of two parents. The keepers are concerned when a newborn foal takes a turn for the worse, and a Sulawesi macaque monkey Rumple gives birth to a baby called Rey, only for the baby to get bullied by some of the higher-ranking females(n)

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Documentary set inside Wood Green animal charity. A two-year-old Pomeranian called Bear arrives at the centre, rescued from a life spent caged on a puppy farm. Can Bear learn to trust again and win the hearts of a family looking for a dog? Meanwhile, Knobby the Chihuahua puppy tries to charm his way into the affections and home of couple Alex and Kier(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets entrepreneur Paul Wilkinson and his wife Amy, who are building a giant new home on a 16-acre plot in Lincolnshire. The superstructure will be comprised of five circular buildings made up of interlocking timber cylinders, linked by raised wooden walkways that hang over a fishing lake. The complicated build proves difficult for Paul's local team, and their ambitious schedule of completing the project in a year soon looks out of reach(n)

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Egypt's Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Archaeologists find new evidence about how the ancient Egyptians prepared for the afterlife, including a sacred text found in the tomb of the great pharaoh Seti I which was believed to guarantee life after death. An expedition unearths a sealed tomb that has lain untouched for 4000 years, and discovers disturbing evidence of the lengths grave robbers will go to in search of treasure(n)

programme.title: Britain at Low Tide
programme.desc: Palaeobiologist Dr Tori Herridge and her team of experts explore the north coast of Kent around Whitstable. The programme takes a look at the excavation of one of the most significant shipwreck discoveries of recent times, the origins of a strange sandbar, and a forgotten military base in an unusual location which proved pivotal in deciding the outcome of the First World War. Plus, a look at how rising sea levels and coastal erosion mean that a great deal of archaeology may be lost before it can be documented(n)

programme.title: Eye in the Sky (2015)
programme.desc: Colonel Katherine Powell's mission to wipe out a terrorist cell in Kenya is complicated when a girl enters the blast radius of the intended drone strike. Powell has to decide between sacrificing the girl's life or letting the terrorists flee to carry out more atrocities. Thriller, starring Helen Mirren, Alan Rickman and Aaron Paul(n)

programme.title: Gods of Egypt (2016)
programme.desc: An ancient Egyptian god is driven into exile when his evil brother murders their father and seizes his throne. He enlists the aid of a human thief in restoring justice, and in return offers to help the mortal to bring his murdered lover back to life. Fantasy adventure, starring Nikolaj Coster-Waldau, Brenton Thwaites, Gerard Butler and Chadwick Boseman(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)
